compiled_state.title: test-flock-busybox-compat: PATH-stripping filter may not remove flock on systems where it lives in a non-flock-named directory
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description: In test_ticket_create_without_flock, the PATH filter uses grep -v 'util-linux|flock' which only removes path components whose directory name contains 'flock' or 'util-linux'. On systems where flock lives in /usr/bin, the filter silently fails and the test runs with real flock in PATH. A more reliable fix is to strip by binary name or construct a minimal PATH from safe directories.

comments.body: Classification: behavioral, Score: 1 (BASIC). Same root cause as ae8d-55be — PATH filter silently fails when flock is in a non-flock-named directory like /usr/bin. Both bugs will be fixed together with the same patch.
comments.author: Test
